info.title: osModa Spawn API
info.description:
Public API for spawning and controlling osModa AI-agent servers.

Spawn a dedicated cloud VM running a root-capable AI agent, pay per spawn in USDC via x402, then poll status, chat over WebSocket, and observe a server-event SSE stream — all with the `osk_` Bearer token returned by spawn.

Quick start: GET /api/v1/plans → POST /api/v1/spawn/{plan} (x402) → GET /api/v1/status/{orderId} until running → WS /api/v1/chat/{orderId}?token=osk_…

Every response carries X-Request-Id. Errors share a uniform envelope (code, message, request_id). Per-token limits: 10 spawns/h, 120 status/min, 3 concurrent chat sessions; 429s include Retry-After. Spawn is idempotent via the Idempotency-Key header (24h).

tags.name: Plans
tags.description: List available server plans and pricing.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Check provisioning status and server details.

tags.name: Chat (WebSocket)
tags.description: Real-time chat with the spawned server's agent. Open as `wss://`. Frame protocol documented under the `x-websocket` extension at the bottom of this spec.

tags.name: Spec-Kit
tags.description: Spec-driven development surface — discover spec-kit projects across spawned servers. Backed by per-server `spec_kit_init` + `spec_kit_run` MCP tools (github/spec-kit, 92K stars). See spawn.os.moda/docs#/Spec-Kit.

get.description: Returns all plans with x402 pricing per supported chain.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

post.description: 1 agent, light tasks — 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM, 40GB SSD. Payment via x402 (USDC).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Opaque client-generated key to make retries safe. Same key + same body returns the same response for 24h. Same key + different body returns 409.

200.description: Server spawning. Returns order ID and API token.

Idempotent-Replayed.description: Present when this response is a cached replay of a prior Idempotency-Key.

example.message: Server provisioning. osModa installs in 5-10 minutes.

400.description: Validation failed (bad plan, invalid Idempotency-Key, etc).

402.description: Payment required. Response body contains x402 payment requirements.

409.description: Idempotency-Key was reused with a different request body.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

post.description: 2-4 agents, real work — 4 vCPU, 8GB RAM, 80GB SSD. Payment via x402 (USDC).

get.description: Returns basic status without auth; full details (server_ip, chat_url, provision_steps, install_error, etc.) when a valid Bearer osk_ token for this order is provided. Poll every 15 s while `status=provisioning`. Surface `provision_steps[]` for live install progress; surface `install_error` on `install_failed`.

install_error.reason: Install exited with code 137 at phase build (OOM)

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

get.description: Returns expiry, creation, and revocation metadata. A token can only read its own metadata.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — token does not own this resource.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

delete.description: Revokes the token permanently. A token can only revoke itself. Subsequent authenticated calls with that token will return 401 with code=token_revoked.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: Forbidden — token does not own this resource.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

get.summary: WebSocket chat with the server's agent (HTTP→WS upgrade)
get.description: **This is a WebSocket endpoint.** Open it as `wss://spawn.os.moda/api/v1/chat/{orderId}?token=osk_…`. It is listed here as a `GET` because OpenAPI 3.0 doesn't natively express WebSocket — see the **`x-websocket`** extension at the bottom of this spec for the full frame protocol, close codes, and message schemas. Try-it from Swagger UI is not supported (Swagger speaks HTTP, not WS); use any WS client such as `wscat -c 'wss://spawn.os.moda/api/v1/chat/<orderId>?token=osk_…'` or the browser `WebSocket` API.  Auth via `token` query parameter. Tokens are validated pre-upgrade; expired/revoked tokens are rejected with `401`/`403` plus an `X-Auth-Reason` response header. Concurrency cap of 3 sessions per token, max 64 KB per frame, 30 s server pings, 10 min idle close (4003), backpressure pause/ resume control frames when buffered output exceeds 1 MB.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The osk_-prefixed Bearer token returned by spawn.

101.description: Switching Protocols → WebSocket established. See x-websocket for frame protocol.

401.description: Missing or malformed token (X-Auth-Reason header set).

403.description: Expired/revoked token, or order not owned by this token.

429.description: Too many concurrent sessions for this token (X-Auth-Reason: too_many_connections).

get.summary: List spec-kit projects across spawned servers
get.description:
Returns all spec-driven-development projects on the caller's servers, aggregated from heartbeat data. Each project surfaces its slug, current phase status, last implement timestamp, and the spec.md path on the source server.

Use case for SaaS integrators: enumerate every active spec-driven feature across a customer's fleet without SSHing in. Maps to the YC 'make your company queryable' principle.

Bearer (osk_) required — token scopes to its own order. To fan out across multiple orders, hold one token per order.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

get.description: Public, unauthenticated agent identity + capability discovery card. Used by other AI agents that want to invoke this one. Contains skills (one per spawn plan), x402 pricing, supported channels, runtime drivers, and capability flags. **Always read this first** to discover the API version, supported runtimes, and `runtimes[].supported_auth_types` (the source of truth for credential / runtime compatibility).

example.name: osModa Spawn
example.description: Spawn dedicated AI-managed servers. Pay with USDC via x402. v1.2.2…

runtimes.name: claude-code
runtimes.description: Anthropic's official Claude CLI. Accepts OAuth + API key.

runtimes.name: openclaw
runtimes.description: OpenClaw multi-runtime CLI (BYOK). API key only.

get.summary: Subscribe to the unified server event stream (SSE)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Replay everything past this id. Use 0 to replay all stored events.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Comma-separated event-type prefixes (e.g. `request,state,agent,heartbeat,install`). Match by leading segment - `request` matches `request.accepted` etc.

get.description: v1.3.0 - Server-Sent Events stream of every async lifecycle event for this server: request.accepted/progress/completed/failed/superseded, state.changed, agent.wedged/healed, heartbeat.received, install.progress. Cursor-resumable, 15 s keepalive comment frames, 30 min hard timeout. Each event has a stable `id` so reconnects can resume cleanly.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: `forbidden` - token does not match this order.

get.description: v1.3.0 - History of recent async actions on this server. Each receipt carries status, accepted_at, completed_at, progress, result, failure, and `_links` to the events stream + this receipt.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: `forbidden` - token does not match this order.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

get.description: v1.3.0 - Poll a single request without subscribing to SSE. Use this if your client can't hold an event stream open.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: `forbidden` - token does not match this order.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

get.summary: Replay persisted chat (survives navigation + tab close)

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Replay events past this id. 0 = full history.

get.description: v1.3.0 - Cold-load chat history captured server-side regardless of WebSocket connection state. Returns the same shape the dashboard chat replays on mount: user messages, agent final replies, and tool-use/tool-result bubbles. Cap: last 200 events. Use this on tab open before connecting WS so your integrator UI shows continuity even after the user navigated away.

cursor.description: Highest event id in this response. Pass as `?since=N` next time.

truncated.description: True if response was capped to 200.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: `forbidden` - token does not match this order.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

get.summary: NDJSON event log for self-serve diagnosis (v1.3.1)

parameters.name: since_ms
parameters.description: Only events with `ts >= this unix-ms`. Pass back the previous `cursor_ms` for incremental polling.

agent_wedged.description: Currently flagged as wedged by the v1.3.1 detector.

auto_restart_attempts.description: How many auto-restart attempts the wedge detector has fired since the current wedge began. Resets to 0 on recovery.

auto_restart_status.description: Status of the most recent attempt. `exhausted` = no further auto-restarts; operator action needed.

truncated.description: True if there were more matching events than `limit`.

cursor_ms.description: Pass to `?since_ms=N` on the next call for incremental polling.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: `forbidden` - token does not match this order.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: `forbidden` - token does not match this order.

post.summary: Set / rotate the AI API key delivered to the agent

202.description: Accepted for delivery. Subscribe to `/events?filter=request,state` to watch progress.

401.description: Missing, expired, or revoked Bearer token.

403.description: `forbidden` - token does not match this order.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until next retry is allowed.

bearerAuth.description: Issued at spawn time. 1-year default TTL. Revocable via DELETE /api/v1/tokens/{token_id}.

RequestReceipt.description: v1.3.0 unified request receipt. Returned by the request endpoints and matches the `request.*` event payloads on the SSE stream.

progress.description: Free-form progress payload. For chat_message the `stage` is `tool_use` or `tool_result`.

failure.description: Set on `failed`. `code` is stable; `fallback_recommendation` is a short hint for the UI.

status.description: GET this URL to re-poll the receipt.

events.description: GET this URL with Accept: text/event-stream to subscribe live.

code.description: Machine-readable error code. Match against this — message wording may change.

request_id.description: Server-assigned request identifier — echoed in X-Request-Id header.

error.description: Legacy alias for `code`. Will be removed in v2.

ssh_key.description: SSH public key (ed25519, RSA, or ECDSA). Injected into the provisioned server.

ai_provider.description: Legacy single-credential field. Use `credentials[]` + `runtime` for the v1.2 modular runtime. If both are present, `credentials[]` wins.

api_key.description: API key for the selected ai_provider. Legacy form — auto-migrated into `credentials[]`. Not persisted server-side; passed once via cloud-init.

runtime.description: Agent runtime. v1.2 modular runtime — pluggable drivers selected per-agent.

credentials.description: Pre-configured credentials. The new server boots with credentials.json.enc populated — no SSH needed to add the first key.

SpawnCredentialInput.description: One pre-configured credential to seed into the new server's encrypted credential store.

secret.description: The actual token. Sent over TLS, stored AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest, never returned via any API.

base_url.description: Optional custom base URL (e.g. for an LLM proxy). HTTPS only; loopback / RFC1918 / metadata IPs rejected to prevent SSRF.

api_token.description: osk_-prefixed bearer token. Store securely — not retrievable again.

chat_url.description: WebSocket URL. Authenticate via `?token=osk_...` query parameter. Frame protocol documented under the `x-websocket` extension at the bottom of this spec.

example.message: Server provisioning. osModa installs in 5-10 minutes.

status.description: One of: pending | provisioning | running | failed | install_failed | deleted

setup_complete.description: True after the spawned server's first heartbeat. Until then the server is mid-install.

last_heartbeat.description: ISO timestamp of the most recent heartbeat from the spawned server.

install_failed_at.description: Set when status flipped to install_failed (either by an explicit /api/provision-failed callback or by the 25-min watchdog cron).

stalled.description: Set by the smart watchdog when the install has been stuck in the same phase for ≥7 min but is still within the 25-min hard timeout. Surfaces a soft-warning state to integrators so the UI can show 'we noticed it's slow, investigating…' instead of leaving the customer staring at a spinner. Cleared automatically when the next phase reports progress.

step.description: Phase the install is stuck on.

stuck_for_min.description: Whole minutes since the phase began.

since.description: Timestamp of the last phase event.

step.description: Phase identifier (preflight, dependencies, build, services, etc.)

InstallError.description: Set on `StatusResponseFull` when `status=install_failed`. Surfaces the most actionable info we have without operator SSHing in.

step.description: Phase that failed (e.g. 'preflight', 'build', 'services', or 'no_callback' when the watchdog flagged it).

log_tail.description: Last ~200 lines of /var/log/osmoda-cloud-init.log. Only present when /api/provision-failed was called explicitly (not from watchdog).

watchdog.description: True if flagged by the spawn-side install-timeout watchdog (no callback received) rather than an explicit failure callback.

example.reason: Install exited with code 137 at phase build (OOM killer)

token_id.description: First 16 hex chars of the token's SHA-256 hash. Safe to log.

X402PaymentRequired.description: x402 payment requirements envelope. See https://x402.org for the protocol. Wrap your fetch with `@x402/fetch` or use the Coinbase CDP SDK to handle this automatically.

step.description: Phase identifier — currently one of: preflight, dependencies, clone, build, openclaw, bridge, workspaces, apikey, services, nixos, reboot. Truncated at 64 chars.

SpecKitProject.description: One spec-driven-development project on a spawned server. Heartbeat data — refreshed every 60 s.

order_id.description: Which spawned server this project lives on.

spec_path.description: Path to spec.md on the source server.

ChatEvent.description: v1.2.5 — one envelope discriminated by `type`. Emitted by `/chat-stream` (SSE) and aggregated by `/chat-history` (JSON). Order is monotonic by `id`. Terminal types are `done` and `error`; clients drop the SSE connection on either.

id.description: Monotonic cursor. Use as the `cursor` query on reconnect.

args_preview.description: First chars of tool args (preview only).

result_preview.description: First chars of tool result (preview only).

tokens_in.description: Optional, present on `done` if the agent reported it.

tokens_out.description: Optional, present on `done` if the agent reported it.

reason.description: Free-form failure reason — typically `Install exited with code N at phase X`. JSON-escaped.

log_tail.description: Last 200 lines of /var/log/osmoda-cloud-init.log, newlines preserved as \n.

AgentCardRuntime.description: One runtime driver advertised by the agent card.

recommended.description: True for the default driver new spawns boot with.

supported_auth_types.description: Credential types accepted. `claude-code` accepts both; `openclaw` accepts api_key only.

default_models.description: Default model IDs offered for this driver. The dashboard adds a master-list fallback so newly-released models surface even on older customer gateways.

AgentCardCapabilities.description: Capability flags advertised by the spawn agent card. Booleans indicate support; numeric/string values carry version or threshold info.

x402.description: x402 payment protocol supported on spawn endpoints.

streaming.description: Server supports streaming responses (SSE / WS chat frames).

modular_runtime.description: v1.2+ pluggable runtime driver per agent.

oauth_credentials.description: Accepts OAuth (sk-ant-oat01-…) credentials, gated by runtime supported_auth_types.

token_lifecycle.description: GET / DELETE /api/v1/tokens/{id} for self-managed token rotation.

structured_errors.description: All errors use the Error envelope with code / request_id.

install_failure_visibility.description: v1.2.1+ — install_failed status + install_error field on full status.

install_watchdog_minutes.description: Minutes after which orders without a heartbeat flip to install_failed.

spec_driven_development.description: v1.2.2+ — github/spec-kit baked into every spawn.

spec_kit_version.description: Pinned spec-kit version installed on every spawn.

network_mode.description: USDC payment network mode currently advertised.

AgentCard.description: ERC-8004 / A2A agent card. Public, unauthenticated. Mirrors `/.well-known/agent-card.json`. Use this to discover the deployed contract: capability flags, available runtime drivers + auth types, the v1 endpoint catalog, and the spawn skill set.

version.description: API version this card was minted from.

runtimes.description: Available runtime drivers. The `supported_auth_types` field on each entry is the source of truth for credential / runtime compatibility.

skills.description: One skill per spawn plan. Each carries x402 price + input/output schemas.

payment.description: Aggregate x402 payment options across all spawn skills.

endpoints.description: Public endpoint catalog. spec_kit_projects added in v1.2.2.

callback_endpoints.description: Endpoints called BY the spawned server, NOT by API integrators.

x-websocket.description: Real-time bidirectional channels. Use these in addition to the REST endpoints above. Message frames are JSON; binary frames are not used.

endpoints.description: Live chat with the spawned server's agent. Authenticate via `?token=osk_...` query parameter (the same Bearer used on REST). Frame protocol: client sends `{type:'chat', text:'…'}` or `{type:'abort'}`; server streams back `status`, `text`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`, `done`, `error`, plus `backpressure_pause`/`backpressure_resume` when buffered output crosses 1 MB / drops below 256 KB.

close_codes.4003: Idle timeout (10 min of no client messages).
close_codes.4008: Concurrency limit (max 3 sessions per token; usually returned pre-upgrade as HTTP 429 with X-Auth-Reason: too_many_connections).

backpressure.behavior: drop frames while paused, do not buffer
